mission.title: Install and Configure kpt on Kubernetes
mission.description: kpt is a package-centric toolchain that enables a WYSIWYG configuration authoring, automation, and delivery experience for Kubernetes. It simplifies managing Kubernetes platforms and KRM-driven infrastructure at scale by treating configuration as data.

steps.title: Install the kpt CLI
steps.description:
Download and install the kpt CLI binary for your platform.
```bash
# Linux amd64
curl -fsSL https://github.com/kptdev/kpt/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.61.1/kpt_linux_amd64 -o kpt
chmod +x kpt
sudo mv kpt /usr/local/bin/

# Verify installation
kpt version
```

steps.title: Initialize a kpt package
steps.description:
Create a new kpt package or fetch an existing one from a Git repository.
```bash
mkdir my-package && cd my-package
kpt pkg init
```

steps.title: Fetch a sample kpt package
steps.description:
Fetch a sample package from the kpt repository to explore configuration management.
```bash
kpt pkg get https://github.com/kptdev/kpt.git/package-examples/helloworld-set@main
cat helloworld-set/Kptfile
```

steps.title: Deploy a kpt package to Kubernetes
steps.description:
Use kpt live to deploy the package to your Kubernetes cluster with resource inventory tracking.
```bash
kpt live init helloworld-set
kpt live apply helloworld-set
```

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
Check the status of the deployed resources.
```bash
kpt live status helloworld-set
kubectl get all
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the kpt CLI available and able to fetch, render, and deploy packages to Kubernetes. The live apply feature provides resource inventory tracking and pruning capabilities.
codeSnippets:
curl -fsSL https://github.com/kptdev/kpt/releases/download/v1.0.0-beta.61.1/kpt_linux_amd64 -o kpt
chmod +x kpt
sudo mv kpt /usr/local/bin/

kpt live init helloworld-set
kpt live apply helloworld-set

uninstall.title: Remove deployed packages
uninstall.description: Use kpt live destroy to remove resources deployed by kpt.

upgrade.title: Download the latest kpt binary
upgrade.description: Download the latest release binary from the kpt GitHub releases page.

upgrade.title: Update deployed packages
upgrade.description: Use kpt pkg update to pull the latest version of packages and re-apply.

troubleshooting.title: kpt live apply fails with inventory error
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure you ran kpt live init before apply. Check the ResourceGroup inventory.
```bash
kpt live init my-package --force
```

troubleshooting.title: Package fetch fails
troubleshooting.description: Verify the Git URL and branch/tag reference. Ensure git is installed and the repository is accessible.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with kubectl and git installed.
